title: Jesus and the Wisdom Figure of Proverbs 8
author: heiser

entries.title: Proverbs 8 — Wisdom as a Co-Creator Figure Identified with Jesus
entries.content_summary: Proverbs 8:22-31 describes the wisdom of God as a person/entity — a deity-level figure who assists God with creation. New Testament authors identify Jesus with this Wisdom figure. In Luke 11:49-51, Jesus quotes 'the Wisdom of God' as a separate speaker who says 'I will send them prophets and apostles.' But in the parallel passage Matthew 23:34, Jesus puts those same words into his OWN mouth — 'Therefore I send you prophets and wise men.' Luke and Matthew together identify Jesus as God's co-creator Wisdom, who was in turn identified as Yahweh, the God of Israel.
entries.content_detail: The writer of Hebrews also identifies Jesus with Wisdom. In Hebrews 1:3, Jesus is called the 'radiance' (Greek: apaugasma) of the glory of God. This extremely rare word occurs in only one other place — Wisdom of Solomon 7:26, where Wisdom is 'a reflection (apaugasma) of eternal light.' The writer of Hebrews deliberately used this term to identify Jesus with the Wisdom figure. Colossians 1:15-17 and John 1:1-3 also identify Jesus as the pre-existent agent of creation — the Wisdom of Proverbs 8. This identification means Jesus is not merely a created being or first creation — He is the eternal Wisdom attribute of God personified.
